prompt:
Check-in cards for the campground are dead in the water this morning. Someone normalized the script headers across the repo last night ("strict mode first, always" per the new style doc) and now pitchcard.ps1 won't even start:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./pitchcard.ps1 -Path pitches.csv
    MetadataError: pitchcard.ps1:8
    Line |
       8 |      [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path,
         |      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
         | The attribute cannot be added because variable Season with value  would
         | no longer be valid.

That parameter declaration is character-for-character the same as in a dozen other scripts here that run fine, and the message is about Season while pointing at the Path line, which is doing my head in.

While you're in there: the cards themselves have been off for a bit longer than that, and I'd assumed it was bad data until now. On plain standard days every fee prints with the peak surcharge baked in, and the ready line at the bottom of each card comes out as gibberish like 'pitch @{id=A4; zone=river; party=4; nights=3}.id ready' instead of 'pitch A4 ready'. The math and the wording were both right a few weeks ago.

Get it starting and printing correct cards again. pwsh -NoProfile -File test_pitchcard.ps1 must pass and stays untouched.
notes: 6P10 item 2 (5S-style statement-vs-expression confusion; probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16). Defect 1: the style sweep hoisted Set-StrictMode/$ErrorActionPreference ABOVE param(), so the block silently stops being a param block -- 'param' becomes an ordinary command whose parenthesized argument is evaluated as expressions, and applying [ValidateSet] to the not-yet-assigned $Season throws MetadataError 'variable Season with value  would no longer be valid' pointing at the $Path line (probed verbatim; with EAP already Stop the script dies there, exit 1). The raw dump is maximally misleading: it indicts an attribute that is fine everywhere else. Defect 2: `if ($Season = 'peak')` -- assignment where -eq was meant (PowerShell has no ==); always-truthy AND clobbers $Season, so standard days print peak surcharges. Defect 3: `"pitch $p.id ready"` -- member access needs a $() subexpression inside double quotes, so the whole row stringifies followed by literal '.id'. Chain-reveal (documented): defects 2 and 3 are invisible until defect 1 is fixed because nothing past line 8 executes. Reference fix: move param() back to the top, -eq, $($p.id); tests pin standard fees, peak fees, ValidateSet rejection (exit nonzero, stdout empty), and the missing-file 66 contract byte-exact. Differentiation vs wave-5 sibling zsh-scanrun/c-ventplan (5S statement-level syntax): PowerShell-native pins are param()-block placement legality (no bash/zsh/c analog -- a demoted param block half-executes as expressions rather than erroring at parse) and = vs -eq semantics in a language whose if-condition accepts assignment statements. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
